directive.title: Minimal viable DB connection with health check endpoint

auditor.summary: The generated code correctly implements the Zero Trust database connection layer with fail-closed secret enforcement, proper pool configuration, and a well-structured health endpoint with latency tracking. However, the build has two CRITICAL issues: (1) '@neondatabase/serverless' is not in the installed packages, making the code uncompilable, and (2) test files use path aliases without Jest configuration, preventing test execution. These BUILDABILITY violations make the build non-shippable despite the code logic being correct. The code structure and logic are sound, but the dependency and configuration gaps must be resolved before this can pass audit.

top_issues.description: Imports '@neondatabase/serverless' which is NOT listed in package.json dependencies or devDependencies. This violates BUILDABILITY criterion - every import must resolve to an existing package or relative path.
Test file uses path alias '@/' (e.g., '@/lib/db/connection') which requires Jest moduleNameMapper configuration. Without this configuration, tests will fail to resolve imports and cannot run.
Test file uses require() instead of import statements, which may cause issues with TypeScript strict mode and module resolution. The directive requires TypeScript strict mode satisfaction.
